originalTitle: Australia news live: Qantas apologises for four-hour wait on Sydney tarmac; Barnaby Joyce unleashes on dog in 7.30 interview
content: Staff numbers at the Sydney Morning Herald and The Age will be cut by 30 in another round of redundancies announced by Nine’s publishing arm today. The managing director of publishing Tory Maguire called staff to a town hall meeting at 10.30am on Tuesday to unveil what she said was structural change in a digital-first era. McGuire said in an email seen by Guardian Australia: The best way to protect our quality journalism is to ensure our organisation adapts to a changing media landscape, that we’re growing commercially and investing in new areas which connect with new audiences. In particular we need to keep shifting towards the digital-first future. Earlier this year Nine made a number of roles in its broadcasting division redundant. This new round of redundancies will not impact the Australian Financial Review. US Secretary of State Marco Rubio was scheduled to land in Manila on Tuesday morning for the Asean conference, joining Australian foreign minister Penny Wong and their international counterparts, as the rekindled war with Iran is placing traditional American allies under economic strain. To give some international context to the talks, the import-dependent Philippines declared a national energy emergency in March and has been forced to cast an ever-wider net for fuel, including purchases of oil from Russia, whose Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov will arrive Tuesday as well. Before departing from Washington on Monday, Rubio said he was open to the possibility of sideline meetings with both Lavrov and China’s top diplomat Wang Yi, who Beijing only officially confirmed would attend on Monday. A meeting with Wang would come days after US President Donald Trump accused Beijing of meddling in US elections. The week is also expected to see renewed efforts towards regional energy security, including the ongoing push for ratification of the bloc’s Framework Agreement on Petroleum Security fuel-sharing scheme. In May, members signed off on a push for an Asean Power Grid, aimed at connecting national electricity networks to enable cross-border power trading. Rubio will meet the Philippines president Ferdinand Marcos Jr as part of a strengthened US partnership with the Philippines, with the disputed South China Sea certain to be on the agenda. Chinese and Philippine ships regularly clash in the crucial waterway, which Beijing claims nearly in its entirety, despite an international ruling that its assertion has no legal basis. The Philippines has previously said it hoped to conclude a Code of Conduct between Asean and China before ending its year as the bloc’s rotating chair, but analysts have suggested any deal Beijing would be willing to sign would be toothless. - With AFP A Melbourne synagogue was unsuccessful in applications for government grants for security before a December 2024 arson attack that has seen its doors closed ever since. The royal commission’s fifth block of hearings, which are being held in Melbourne this week, is examining security arrangements for the Jewish community. Yesterday, counsel assisting, Richard Lancaster SC, said evidence would show “a substantial and ongoing threat” to the security of the Jewish community, leading to a “shrinking of Jewish communal life”. Today’s first witness is Benjamin Klein, a board member of the Adass Israel synagogue, the subject of an arson attack in December 2024. Asked about applications for grant funding for security in 2023 and 2024 before the attack, he said: There were letters sent out to both government, local members, to different parts of the Jewish of the community seeking grants. It was local council, there was state government, there were federal grants, which we saw were available. We’d sent out letters and were unsuccessful. We also sent out a letter to Mark Dreyfus at the time, who was the attorney general, and we weren’t successful. There were technicalities in terms of incorporated entities, unincorporated entities. There was a lot of red tape to sort of get through and we weren’t successful. The commission has heard that the synagogue’s board has since had to employ someone to oversee funding applications for security grants. After the attack, the federal government has pledged up to $30m to rebuild the synagogue. As we mentioned earlier, the foreign affairs minister, Penny Wong, is in the Philippines today for the Asean foreign affairs ministers’ meeting, the East Asia summit foreign ministers’ meeting and the Asean regional forum. The 11 Asean nations include Indonesia, Malaysia, the Philippines, Singapore, Thailand, Brunei Darussalam, Vietnam, Laos, Myanmar, Cambodia and Timor-Leste. Crucially, it means Wong could have an opportunity to put pressure on Lao officials after they said they do not have enough evidence to lay charges over the methanol poisoning deaths of Australian citizens Holly Bowles and Bianca Jones – because autopsies were not carried out. In a statement on Friday, Wong said the government was “deeply frustrated and bitterly disappointed that authorities in Laos are not pursuing the most serious charges” in relation to the deaths. Labor announces $25m in support for Gaza The federal government has announced a $25m package of support for aid in Gaza and “reforms for governance” in Palestine, “to strengthen the institutional foundations of the State of Palestine”. The foreign affairs minister, Penny Wong, and the minister for international development, Anne Aly, announced the funding this morning, saying it forms part of “coordinated international efforts to advance a two-state solution.” The funding includes $15m to the UN Horizon Fund, whose strategic goal is to “contribute to inclusive early recovery and reconstruction for Palestinians in the Gaza Strip” after the 10 October ceasefire last year. More than $80m has been pledged to the Horizon Fund internationally, with contributions from France, Germany, Italy, the Netherlands, Norway, Sweden, Switzerland and the United Kingdom, the government’s m
chineseTitle: 澳大利亚新闻直播：澳洲航空为在悉尼停机坪上等待四个小时而道歉；巴纳比乔伊斯在 7.30 采访中向狗释放
chineseContent: 九号台的出版部门今天宣布了另一轮裁员，《悉尼先驱晨报》和《时代报》的员工人数将减少 30 人。周二上午 10 点 30 分，出版业董事总经理托里·马奎尔 (Tory Maguire) 召集员工参加市政厅会议，公布她所说的数字优先时代的结构性变革。麦奎尔在澳大利亚卫报看到的一封电子邮件中表示：保护我们高质量新闻的最佳方式是确保我们的组织适应不断变化的媒体格局，我们正在发展商业并投资于与新受众建立联系的新领域。我们特别需要不断转向数字优先的未来。今年早些时候，九号台裁减了其广播部门的一些职位。新一轮裁员不会影响《澳大利亚金融评论报》。美国国务卿马可·卢比奥定于周二上午抵达马尼拉，与澳大利亚外长黄英贤和国际同行一起参加东盟会议，因为与伊朗的战争重新燃起，使美国的传统盟友面临经济压力。为了给会谈提供一些国际背景，依赖进口的菲律宾在三月份宣布国家能源紧急状态，并被迫扩大燃料网，包括从俄罗斯购买石油，俄罗斯外交部长谢尔盖·拉夫罗夫也将于周二抵达。在周一离开华盛顿之前，卢比奥表示，他对与拉夫罗夫和中国最高外交官王毅举行场外会晤的可能性持开放态度，北京方面周一才正式确认将出席王毅的会面。几天前，美国总统唐纳德·特朗普指责北京干预美国大选，与王毅会面。预计本周还将针对该地区做出新的努力
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
